_comment: S3b Negative-DT Boundary — companion attribute study to S3 (R&R-003, GitHub #1). Defined 2026-06-06.
name: Negative-DT decode boundary
description: 10 parts × 2 appraisers × 3 trials. Companion to the redesigned S3: sweeps DT from 0.0 down to −2.7 s in 0.3 s steps and measures DECODE RATE (attribute: did the message decode? yes/no) rather than DT precision. Cleanly separates the question 'does it decode?' from 'does it report DT accurately?' — both questions were conflated in the original S3 design. The product finding from run aa053a9 is that OpenWSFZ still decodes most negative-DT signals (decode rate ≈ near-perfect even for parts 0–3) but reports DT ≈ 0 regardless of the true offset; this study measures the decode half of that finding. Analysis: per-part decode rate per appraiser; reported as an informational table, not a Gage R&R. See STUDY-SPEC §6 and §16.
harness_note: Negative DT means the transmitter fires before the nominal slot boundary. The harness must schedule playback DT seconds early relative to the slot start. The synthesiser modulate() offset is clamped to 0 for negative values; timing must be enforced at the playback layer (subtract |dt_s| seconds from the cycle boundary before arming playback). Analysis key: matched (decoded) vs missed (not decoded) per (part, appraiser) — DO NOT compute Gage R&R on the DT response for this scenario.
